selected_important_turn.text: Ok, I have enough food, but I need firewood to keep warm and water because one of my containers leaked.  So, why don't you take all the food and I get everything else.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete allocation from YOU and it shifts the dialogue from preference disclosure to a zero-sum proposal that locks resources into a one-sided split. Early offers set the negotiation frame and make integrative solutions harder if they appear rigid.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The move is proself and framed as a firm division ("you take all the food, I get everything else") without exploring the opponent's flexibility or seeking mutually beneficial trades. Even though it produced quick agreement here, it risks making the process seem zero-sum, reduces trust, and forecloses possible creative tradeoffs (e.g., splitting items differently or compensating with other value).
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Shift from a take-it-or-leave-it partition to a tentative, exploratory proposal: acknowledge their stated food priority, express your needs, and invite alternatives (e.g., "I need firewood and water to stay safe — what tradeoffs would you consider so we both get what matters most?"). Offer multiple small options rather than one rigid division.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: Likely increase — opens space for integrative trades and creative splits that can raise combined utility.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: Increase — a collaborative, exploratory framing signals concern for mutual needs rather than seizing value.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: Slightly increase — although it may lengthen the talk a bit, proposals become easier to accept when both sides help shape them.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: Increase — a less aggressive, more invitational approach builds trust and reduces perceived competitiveness.

value2reason.Low: I can get water at other places.
value2reason.Medium: I need to use firewood for warm and heat.
value2reason.High: I need to prepare myself wheneber I go out

value2reason.Low: One of my water bottles broke and is leaking
value2reason.Medium: I only brought the bare minimum but no snacks
value2reason.High: We are camping in the winter and I dont have a heavy jacket

dialogue.text: I have to get food as high while water is low.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ok, I have enough food, but I need firewood to keep warm and water because one of my containers leaked.  So, why don't you take all the food and I get everything else.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your need for firewood and water, but I prioritize food more. Let's split the food evenly, and I'll take two firewood, you take one. For water, I'll take one, you take two. That way, we both get what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need firewood and water for safety, but I'm willing to trade one firewood for one of your food packages if that works for you. Let’s find a balance that covers our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with trading one food for one firewood, but I still need to ensure I get at least two food packages. Let’s adjust: I take two food, one firewood, and one water. You take one food, two firewood, and two water. That covers both our priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need firewood and water for safety, but I'm willing to trade one firewood for one of your food packages if that works for you. Let’s find a balance that covers our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think we can agree on this split: I take two food, one firewood, and one water. You take one food, two firewood, and two water. It balances our priorities and ensures we’re both prepared.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate your proposal, but I need more firewood for warmth and safety. How about I take two firewood, one food, and one water, while you take one firewood, two food, and two water? This way, we both meet our priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
